project.name: @paperclipai/system-garden
project.description: System Garden -- interactive agent dependency graph and health dashboard for the Paperclip AI platform.

nodes.name: constants.ts
nodes.summary: Defines shared constants for the System Garden plugin: plugin identity (ID, version, display name), UI slot IDs and export names, graph node color palette by kind, and health score thresholds with Korean-language labels.

nodes.name: cytoscape.d.ts
nodes.summary: Ambient TypeScript module declaration for the cytoscape library, providing minimal typed interfaces for CytoscapeCore, CytoscapeNode, CytoscapeCollection, and the factory function used to create graph instances.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel entry point that re-exports the plugin manifest, worker plugin instance, and all shared constants, serving as the public API surface of the system-garden package.

nodes.name: manifest.ts
nodes.summary: Declares the Paperclip plugin manifest (v1 API) for System Garden, specifying plugin identity, required capabilities (issues.read, agents.read, UI registrations), entrypoints for the worker and UI bundles, and two UI slots (a full page and a sidebar link).

nodes.name: index.tsx
nodes.summary: React UI layer for System Garden: renders the main dashboard page with a Cytoscape.js interactive graph, health score cards, metacognition question list, and agent detail side panel. Also exports a sidebar navigation link component.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses dynamic import() for lazy-loading the cytoscape library, a module-level singleton promise pattern to avoid duplicate loads, and extensive inline CSSProperties with CSS color-mix() for theming.

nodes.name: worker.ts
nodes.summary: Backend worker that powers the System Garden plugin: fetches agents and issues from the Paperclip SDK, builds an agent dependency graph from reportsTo and chainOfCommand relationships, computes per-agent health scores with completion ratios and failure streaks, generates metacognition questions about idle agents / workload skew / review bottlenecks, and exposes two data endpoints (garden snapshot and agent detail).
nodes.languageNotes: Leverages TypeScript conditional narrowing with Awaited<ReturnType<...>> to derive API record types from the SDK, and uses Set-based status classification for O(1) membership checks.

nodes.name: buildGraph
nodes.summary: Constructs a directed graph of agent nodes and edges from reportsTo hierarchy and chainOfCommand metadata, deduplicating edges and filtering out references to non-existent agents.

nodes.name: buildHealthCards
nodes.summary: Produces health score cards for each agent by combining issue completion ratios, active-status bonuses, review-backlog penalties, and consecutive failure streak penalties into a 0-100 clamped score.

nodes.name: buildMetaQuestions
nodes.summary: Generates metacognition questions (Korean text) about organizational health: idle agents without recent activity, workload concentration exceeding 30%, review bottlenecks with 3+ pending reviews, and open-issue backlogs exceeding completed counts.

nodes.name: buildGardenSnapshot
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the full garden snapshot creation: fetches agents and issues in parallel from the Paperclip SDK, then delegates to buildGraph, buildHealthCards, and buildMetaQuestions to assemble the complete dashboard data.

nodes.name: extractChainTargets
nodes.summary: Extracts chain-of-command target agent IDs from an agent record by checking multiple metadata locations (direct property, metadata object, runtimeConfig) with both camelCase and snake_case keys, deduplicating results.

nodes.name: SystemGardenPage
nodes.summary: Main React page component that wires up the entire System Garden dashboard: subscribes to the garden snapshot data endpoint, initializes a Cytoscape.js graph with interactive hover/tap highlighting, and renders the header, graph panel with agent detail sidebar, health cards section, and metacognition questions section.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses useEffect with an async IIFE pattern for Cytoscape initialization, a cleanup-flag pattern to avoid state updates after unmount, and useMemo for derived agent selection.

nodes.name: buildElements
nodes.summary: Transforms GraphNode and GraphEdge domain objects into the CytoscapeElementPayload format required by the cytoscape library, mapping node kinds and statuses to element data attributes.

nodes.name: HealthCardRow
nodes.summary: React component that renders a responsive grid of health score cards with color-coded backgrounds (green/amber/red) based on score thresholds, showing score value, delta trend indicator, and detail text for each card.

nodes.name: QuestionList
nodes.summary: React component that renders a vertical list of metacognition questions, each showing the question text and an action hint in a styled card format.

nodes.name: SystemGardenSidebarLink
nodes.summary: React component for the sidebar navigation link to the System Garden page, with active-page styling that highlights when the current URL matches the plugin route.

layers.name: Plugin Entry & Manifest
layers.description: Package entry point and Paperclip plugin manifest declaration that define the plugin's identity, capabilities, entrypoints, and public API surface.

layers.name: Shared Constants & Types
layers.description: Shared configuration constants (plugin IDs, color palette, health thresholds) and ambient type declarations for the cytoscape library, consumed by both the worker and UI layers.

layers.name: Worker / Data Layer
layers.description: Backend worker that fetches agents and issues from the Paperclip SDK, constructs the agent dependency graph, computes health scores, and generates metacognition questions -- serving data to the UI via registered data endpoints.

layers.name: UI / Presentation Layer
layers.description: React-based dashboard UI that renders the interactive Cytoscape.js agent graph, health score cards, metacognition questions, and agent detail panels, consuming snapshot data from the worker layer.

tour.title: Package Entry Point
tour.description: Start at src/index.ts, the barrel file that re-exports the manifest, worker plugin, and all constants. This 3-line file is the public API surface of the package -- it tells you that System Garden has three pillars: a plugin manifest, a backend worker, and shared constants.
tour.languageLesson: TypeScript barrel files use 'export { default as X } from' and 'export *' to aggregate and re-export modules, creating a single import target for consumers.

tour.title: Plugin Identity & Registration
tour.description: The manifest declares System Garden as a Paperclip plugin (v1 API). It requests capabilities to read agents and issues, and registers two UI slots: a full-page dashboard and a sidebar navigation link. This is the contract between the plugin and the host application.

tour.title: Shared Constants
tour.description: Constants.ts defines the single source of truth for plugin identity, UI slot IDs, export names, node color palette (agent/schedule/data/output), and health score thresholds with Korean-language labels. Both the worker and UI import from here, ensuring consistency.

tour.title: Type Declarations
tour.description: The cytoscape.d.ts file provides ambient TypeScript types for the Cytoscape.js graph library. Rather than pulling in the full @types package, the plugin declares a minimal typed surface (factory, core, node, collection) tailored to its actual usage patterns.
tour.languageLesson: TypeScript 'declare module' blocks create ambient type declarations for untyped or minimally-typed libraries, letting you define just the API surface you actually use.

tour.title: Worker: Data & Graph Construction
tour.description: The worker is the backend engine. It starts by fetching all agents and issues from the Paperclip SDK in parallel, then buildGraph() creates nodes for each agent and edges from reportsTo and chainOfCommand relationships. The extractChainTargets helper normalizes metadata across multiple locations and naming conventions.

tour.title: Worker: Health Scoring
tour.description: Building on the graph from Step 5, buildHealthCards() computes a 0-100 health score for each agent. The algorithm combines issue completion ratios with status bonuses, review-backlog penalties, and consecutive-failure-streak penalties. Scores map to Korean health labels: 'prosperous', 'growing', or 'wilting'.

tour.title: Worker: Metacognition Questions
tour.description: The most distinctive feature: buildMetaQuestions() analyzes organizational patterns and surfaces actionable questions. It detects idle agents (no activity in 7 days), workload concentration (>30% of issues on one agent), review bottlenecks (3+ pending reviews), and open-issue backlogs. Each question includes a concrete action hint.

tour.title: Worker: Snapshot Orchestration
tour.description: buildGardenSnapshot() ties everything together: it fetches data, then delegates to buildGraph, buildHealthCards, and buildMetaQuestions to assemble the complete GardenSnapshot. The definePlugin setup registers two data endpoints that the UI subscribes to.

tour.title: UI: Dashboard Page
tour.description: The SystemGardenPage React component renders the full dashboard. It subscribes to the garden snapshot data endpoint using usePluginData, initializes a Cytoscape.js graph with interactive hover/tap highlighting on a useEffect lifecycle, and composes the header, graph panel with agent detail sidebar, HealthCardRow, and QuestionList sections.
tour.languageLesson: The async IIFE inside useEffect with a 'disposed' flag is a common React pattern for preventing state updates after component unmount when an async initialization is in progress.

tour.title: UI: Supporting Components
tour.description: HealthCardRow renders a responsive CSS grid of color-coded score cards with delta trend indicators. QuestionList renders metacognition questions as styled cards. SystemGardenSidebarLink provides sidebar navigation with active-page highlighting. Together they compose the dashboard's visual identity.
